metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring (a) deletion/update of existing tests covering the removed surfaces, and (b) at least one behavioral test per removed surface confirming it now fails with a clear, user-visible error (e.g., CLI returns non-zero with 'unknown command/option', lib raises a named exception, MCP returns an error response).
findings.finding: The plan specifies no test work for the deleted surfaces: no removal of existing tests that covered list_epics/--verdict-hash/--no-sync/REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS/rebar_id_guard_mode/lock_backend='file', and no new tests verifying that callers attempting to use the removed surfaces receive a clear error (rather than silent misbehavior). Deleted behaviors require corresponding modify/remove-test work per the rubric.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (all four AC lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] Tests covering removed surfaces (list_epics CLI/lib/MCP, --verdict-hash, --no-sync, REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS, rebar_id_guard_mode, lock_backend="file") are deleted or updated; a regression test per surface confirms it now returns a clear error to callers rather than silently misbehaving.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the edit-target list all unenumerated consumers of `list_epics` retirement: `mcp_server.py` (remove `ListEpicsOut` import/export), `_mcp_models.py` (remove `ListEpicsOut` class), `schemas/__init__.py` (remove `LIST_EPICS`), `schemas/gen_types.py` (remove `'list_epics'` entry), `_cli/__init__.py` (remove from `_READS_INIT_ONLY`), `_cli/help/list-epics.txt`, 6 golden files under `tests/golden/cli_help/list-epics__*`, and test functions in `test_schema_outputs.py`, `test_mcp_output_schema_coverage.py`, `test_help_overview_coverage.py`, and `test_schema_coverage.py`.
findings.finding: The `list_epics` family retirement requires updating a large set of consumers outside the named source files that are not enumerated in the plan: `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` (imports and re-exports `ListEpicsOut`), `src/rebar/_mcp_models.py` (defines `ListEpicsOut`), `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` (exports `LIST_EPICS`), `src/rebar/schemas/gen_types.py` (lists `'list_epics'`), `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (`_READS_INIT_ONLY` frozenset), `src/rebar/_cli/help/list-epics.txt` (help text), 6 golden test files (`tests/golden/cli_help/list-epics__*`), and test files `test_schema_outputs.py:219-239`, `test_mcp_output_schema_coverage.py:62,279`, `test_help_overview_coverage.py:27`, `test_schema_coverage.py:109`.
findings.location: Scope section / cascade_flag; AC: 'list_epics retired across lib + MCP tool + CLI subcommand + output schema'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit steps to the plan's removal sequence for `list_epics`: update `schemas/gen_types.py` (remove `'list_epics'` from `TOP_LEVEL_OBJECTS`), regenerate `types.py` (removing `class ListEpics`), and remove the `LIST_EPICS` constant and `'list_epics'` wiring entry from `schemas/__init__.py`.
findings.finding: The plan does not address the generated-code cascade triggered by removing `schemas/list_epics.schema.json`: `schemas/gen_types.py` lists `'list_epics'` in its `TOP_LEVEL_OBJECTS` registry, and `types.py` contains the generated `ListEpics` TypedDict. Deleting the schema file without updating `gen_types.py` and regenerating `types.py` will leave a stale TypedDict (or cause the build-time freshness check to fail), and the `schemas/__init__.py` wiring entry `'list_epics': LIST_EPICS` plus `schemas/__init__.py:101` (`LIST_EPICS = 'list_epics'`) also need removal.
findings.location: AC: `list_epics retired across lib + MCP tool + CLI subcommand + output schema`

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For `rebar_id_guard_mode` and `lock_backend="file"`, explicitly name the canonical replacement (or state 'no replacement; simply remove from config') in the plan body and reflect this in AC3's verify step.
findings.finding: AC3 lists `rebar_id_guard_mode` and `lock_backend="file"` as items to remove, but neither the plan body nor any AC names a canonical replacement or migration path for external consumers of these config values. For a migration criterion, both removal AND replacement must be verifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 / Why section citations for `rebar_id_guard_mode` and `lock_backend="file"`
findings.suggested_fix: In the Why citations, add e.g.: '`rebar_id_guard_mode` → simply remove from config (no replacement; guard mode is now unconditional)' and '`lock_backend="file"` → remove from config (only advisory lock backend is now supported)'. Update AC3 to read: 'removed and external consumers directed to canonical replacement or advised to remove the key'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` (lines 240, 254, 258) to the edit targets for `list-epics` removal — remove or replace those three invocations with equivalent `rebar list --type=epic` calls.
findings.finding: `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` contains three invocations of `list-epics` (lines 240, 254, 258) that are not enumerated in the plan's cascade sweep. This is a consumer outside the `src/` directory that will fail or require update once the `list-epics` subcommand is removed.
findings.location: Scope section: 'cascade_flag: removes public CLI/MCP/lib entrypoints + a schema — sweep external references'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC4 as a set of observable outcomes naming each artifact: e.g. 'CHANGELOG contains a BREAKING entry listing all six removed surfaces; README and MCP tool listing no longer reference list-epics/--verdict-hash/etc.; deprecation registry entries for each surface are marked retired with the beta version'.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Deprecation registry + docs + release-notes updated (BREAKING section)') is effort-framed and under-specified: 'updated' does not state what a passing state looks like, which specific doc files must change, or what constitutes a complete BREAKING section entry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default blocking rule explicitly: either 'this ticket is blocked until the registry-completeness ticket is merged and released' or 'if the sibling is not yet released, surfaces that lack a registry entry must add a warning commit in this PR before removal'.
findings.finding: The sequencing dependency on a sibling ticket ('Sequence AFTER the registry-completeness ticket adds any missing warnings') is stated but unresolved: no blocking dependency is formalized and no default action is stated if that ticket is incomplete when this work begins.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific canonical replacement env var for `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS` (e.g., `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERATIONS`) in the plan's What/AC section and ensure the BREAKING release-notes entry states 'replace `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS` with `<exact-canonical-var>`'.
findings.finding: The canonical replacement env var for `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS` is never named — the plan says only '→ canonical `REBAR_LLM_*`' without specifying which variable (e.g., `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERATIONS` or similar). The required BREAKING section in docs/release-notes therefore cannot give users a concrete migration path, leaving them with no way to restore the behaviour they relied on.
findings.location: Why section / `llm/config.py:15,363-371` / AC: 'Deprecation registry + docs + release-notes updated (BREAKING section)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC, change the line to: '`REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS` removed — BREAKING section must specify exact canonical replacement (e.g., `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERATIONS`) and confirm `llm/config.py` exposes it.' Update the Why section similarly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider noting in the plan whether the simpler flag/env/config removals (--verdict-hash, --no-sync, REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS, rebar_id_guard_mode, lock_backend='file') can proceed independently if list_epics work encounters unexpected complexity, or explicitly confirm they must all ship atomically and explain why.
findings.finding: The list_epics removal (spanning lib + MCP tool + CLI subcommand + output schema + grep sweep) is substantially more complex than the other five items (each a single-file flag/env-var/config removal), and there is no internal sequencing guidance. If list_epics hits unexpected coupling or schema-migration concerns, it will block the simpler removals from shipping in the same release — a diffusion risk across unrelated subsystems.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC: e.g. AC1 use `grep -rE '(list_epics|--verdict-hash|REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS|--no-sync|rebar_id_guard_mode|lock_backend[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*["'"'"']file)' src/` with word-boundary anchors; AC2 add `rebar list-epics 2>&1 | grep -i 'not recognized\|no such command'`; AC3 add `rebar transition --verdict-hash X 2>&1 | grep 'unrecognized'`.
findings.finding: No proving commands are specified for any acceptance criterion; AC1's grep-based check lacks anchored patterns, risking substring false-positives (e.g. `grep list_epics` would match comments or unrelated identifiers); ACs 2–4 have no commands at all.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a Verification section with one command per AC:
- AC1: `grep -rEn '\blist_epics\b|--verdict-hash|\bREBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS\b|\b--no-sync\b|\brebar_id_guard_mode\b|lock_backend[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*["']file["']' src/ && echo FAIL || echo PASS`
- AC2: `python -m rebar list-epics 2>&1 | grep -qi 'unknown\|not recognized'; echo $?` (expect 0)
- AC3: For each removed flag, invoke the CLI with the old flag and assert non-zero exit / 'unrecognized argument'
- AC4: `grep -c 'BREAKING' CHANGELOG.md` (expect ≥1 new entry)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ordered steps list (e.g. 1. migrate internal uses, 2. remove each surface by name with file reference, 3. delete schema file, 4. update registry/docs/CHANGELOG) so every AC maps to at least one step.
findings.finding: The plan has no steps section; the 'What' paragraph describes intent but not an ordered sequence of actions, so there is no mapping from steps to ACs and an executor cannot confirm the union of steps reaches the stated end-state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the cascade_flag action: state that external references found inside this repo (tests, fixtures, docs) must be updated as part of this PR, and references in downstream repos should be noted in the BREAKING section but do not block merge.
findings.finding: The cascade_flag directive 'sweep external references' does not specify what action to take when external references are found — document them, fail the PR, open follow-up issues — leaving a real design decision to the executor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (e.g., a git revert strategy, a feature-flag re-enable path, or a documented 'restore shim' procedure) for if the removals cause unexpected breakage post-beta release.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges breaking changes and references expand-contract sequencing (warn-then-remove), but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan if the removals cause unexpected downstream breakage post-beta. The T4 criterion requires a reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection to the plan: e.g., 'If unexpected breakage is discovered post-beta, revert commits X–Y to restore shims; re-issue a deprecation warning release before the next removal attempt. Flag: REBAR_KEEP_DEPRECATED_SURFACES=1 re-enables shims for one release cycle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or implementation note) specifying the post-removal runtime UX for each removed surface: removed CLI subcommands/flags must emit a clear migration hint (exit-code 2 + message naming the replacement); removed env vars and config keys must log a startup error or warning naming the canonical replacement, rather than silently ignoring or producing a generic crash.
findings.finding: The plan specifies which surfaces to remove but does not specify the user-visible error experience when an external consumer invokes a removed surface post-removal (e.g., `rebar list-epics`, `--verdict-hash`, `--no-sync`, `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS`, `lock_backend="file"`, `rebar_id_guard_mode`). Without specifying this, implementers may leave users with cryptic generic errors instead of actionable migration messages.
findings.suggested_fix: For each surface, choose one of: (a) keep a thin shim that prints a migration message and exits non-zero, then deletes itself in a follow-up; or (b) add a config/startup validator that detects the old key/var and emits a named error with the replacement. Add an AC: 'Each removed surface produces a human-readable error citing the replacement when invoked post-removal.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `__init__.py:1056-1090 (lib, warns)` citation: the lib `list_epics` function was already removed pre-1.0; confirm there is no remaining lib-layer warning shim to retire, and update the plan scope accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan cites `lib/__init__.py:1056-1090` as containing a `list_epics` library function that 'warns,' but the file is ~190 lines total and no `list_epics` function exists anywhere in `__init__.py` or is exported from it. Code in `_lib_reads.py:284-291` confirms the lib function was already removed pre-1.0 (comment-only tombstone). The plan commits work to migrate/remove a lib shim that is already gone, potentially leaving implementers hunting for a non-existent surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the lib citation with: '`_lib_reads.py:284-291` (tombstone comment only — lib function already removed pre-1.0; no shim remains). Only the MCP tool and CLI subcommand need removal.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `_mcp_reads.py:239-252 (MCP tool, suppresses warning)` citation: the MCP tool actively emits the deprecation warning (via `warn_deprecated(..., via='warning')`); update the description to reflect this so sequencing assumptions remain valid.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `_mcp_reads.py:239-252` '(MCP tool, suppresses warning)' but the actual code at lines 239–259 actively calls `warn_deprecated('mcp:list_epics', via='warning')` — it emits the warning, it does not suppress it. This is a directly refuted codebase assertion.
findings.suggested_fix: Change '(MCP tool, suppresses warning)' to '(MCP tool, emits deprecation warning via warn_deprecated registry)' — the warning-release precondition is already met.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `--verdict-hash` still requires code removal or only comment/doc cleanup: probe `_commands/transition.py` and `_commands/txn.py` to confirm no explicit parse branch exists, and update the plan to reflect that the flag is already absent from `_parse_flags` (only comment tombstones remain).
findings.finding: The plan cites `_commands/transition.py:45,94-102` as a `--verdict-hash` 'accepted-but-ignored flag,' implying it is a remaining deprecated surface to remove. The actual code at lines 97–99 contains a comment stating the flag 'was removed pre-1.0 — DE7' and 'is now just an unknown token, silently skipped.' The `_parse_flags` function never explicitly parses or accepts `--verdict-hash`; it is already non-existent at the parse layer. The plan may be scheduling removal of a surface that is already gone.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the `rebar_id_guard_mode` citation from `config.py:289-300` to `_engine/rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py:136-173` (`_get_rebar_id_guard_mode_from_config`), and clarify whether any flat-conf reading code still remains to be removed (given the inline comment at line 186 says it was already removed pre-1.0).
findings.finding: The plan cites `config.py:289-300` as the location of the `rebar_id_guard_mode` flat-conf key deprecation, but those lines contain `_discover_project_config`. The actual flat-conf reader for `rebar_id_guard_mode` lives in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py:136-188`, which explicitly states at line 186-188 that 'the flat-conf reader was removed pre-1.0 — DE7.' The plan may be scheduling removal of a surface the codebase already describes as removed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `__init__.py:1056-1090` citation — the lib `rebar.list_epics()` function was already removed pre-1.0; update the plan to reflect that the lib surface is done and enumerate remaining artifacts (test cleanup in `test_schema_outputs.py:219-239`, `_lib_reads.py` comment block).
findings.finding: The plan cites `__init__.py:1056-1090` as the lib `list_epics` function location (described as 'lib, warns'), but `src/rebar/__init__.py` is ~130 lines long and has no `list_epics` export — the function was already removed pre-1.0 (confirmed by `_lib_reads.py:284-292` comment 'the deprecated rebar.list_epics() library function was removed pre-1.0'). This is a stale/hallucinated citation; the lib surface is already gone.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'lib+MCP+CLI `list_epics` family: `__init__.py:1056-1090` (lib, warns)' with 'lib `rebar.list_epics()` already removed (DE7); remaining: MCP tool (`_mcp_reads.py:234-259`), CLI subcommand (`reads_cli.py:507-595`), schema (`schemas/list_epics.schema.json`), associated test/golden artifacts.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `--verdict-hash` removal is already complete (code already silently skips it as unknown token per `transition.py:97`) and update the plan to reflect this — scope the remaining work to documentation/registry updates only, not code removal.
findings.finding: The plan cites `transition.py:45,94-102` as the `--verdict-hash` 'accepted-but-ignored flag' location, but examination of those lines shows `--verdict-hash` is NOT accepted: it was already silently skipped as an unknown token (comment at line 97: 'The --verdict-hash flag was removed pre-1.0 — DE7'). The flag is absent from `_deprecations.py` REGISTRY entirely. There is no shim to remove — the work may already be done.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the `rebar_id_guard_mode` citation: replace `config.py:289-300` with the actual locations `_engine/rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py:136-173` (reader function), `applier.py:210,351` (caller), and `binding_store.py:59` (mirror caller).
findings.finding: The plan cites `config.py:289-300` for the `rebar_id_guard_mode` flat-conf key, but that range contains the `_locate_config_file` function (unrelated to id-guard-mode). The actual implementation reading `rebar_id_guard_mode` is in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py:136-173`, with callers also in `rebar_reconciler/applier.py:210,351` and `rebar_reconciler/binding_store.py:59`.
findings.suggested_fix: Update Why section citation to: 'flat-conf key `rebar_id_guard_mode`: `_engine/rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py:136-173` (reader), `applier.py:210,351` (caller), `binding_store.py:59` (mirror caller).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to edit targets for `--no-sync` removal: the 6 help `.txt` files (`_cli/help/{show,list,search,session-logs,ready,deps}.txt`), their 12 golden snapshot files (`tests/golden/cli_help/{list,show,ready,deps,search,session-logs}__*help.out`), and doc references in `docs/bash-migration.md` and `docs/concurrency.md`.
findings.finding: The `--no-sync` removal touches six CLI help text files (`src/rebar/_cli/help/{show,list,search,session-logs,ready,deps}.txt`) and their corresponding golden test snapshots (12+ files under `tests/golden/cli_help/` for list, show, ready, deps, search, session-logs) — none of which are enumerated in the plan. Additionally `docs/bash-migration.md` and `docs/concurrency.md` mention `--no-sync` as a user-facing flag.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the cited location for `flat rebar_id_guard_mode` removal from `config.py:289-300` to `_engine/rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py:136-173` (`_get_rebar_id_guard_mode_from_config`), and confirm whether this function is dead code (given the contradicting docstring in `_resolve_id_guard_bypass`) or a live reader that must be excised along with its import in `applier.py`.
findings.finding: The plan's cited location for `flat rebar_id_guard_mode` removal (`config.py:289-300`) points to the unrelated `_discover_project_config` function; the live code that still reads this flat key is `rebar_id_audit.py:136-173` (`_get_rebar_id_guard_mode_from_config()`), which is actively imported and called by `applier.py`. If the implementer follows only the cited location, they will miss the live reader and leave the flat-key read path in place, making the AC for 'flat `rebar_id_guard_mode` removed' silently unachievable.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — citation `config.py:289-300` and AC line `--verdict-hash, --no-sync, REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS, flat rebar_id_guard_mode, and lock_backend="file" removed`
findings.suggested_fix: Update the plan's citation to `_engine/rebar_reconciler/rebar_id_audit.py:136-173` and `applier.py:210,351`, and add an explicit AC step: verify `_get_rebar_id_guard_mode_from_config` is deleted and no longer imported.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
